sources.issuing_organisation: American Academy of Otolaryngology–Head and Neck Surgery Foundation
sources.exact_document_title: Clinical Practice Guideline: Benign Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo (Update)
sources.jurisdiction: United States specialty guideline; international evidence requiring UAE vestibular and referral-pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adults aged 18 years and older with suspected, diagnosed, monitored, or managed benign paroxysmal positional vertigo.
sources.clinical_setting: Any clinical setting in which adult BPPV is identified, monitored, or managed.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for adult positional-symptom assessment, modifying factors, fall-risk context, reassessment, persistent symptoms, education, and clinician-arranged follow-up. It does not diagnose BPPV, select a manoeuvre, recommend imaging, or prescribe vestibular suppressants.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 1a–3 — positional assessment, differential, and modifying factors
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting positional symptom context, clinician-performed diagnostic assessment, mobility, central nervous system context, home support, fall risk, and factors affecting management without asserting BPPV.

exact_sections.heading: Key action statements 7–8b — reassessment, persistent symptoms, and education
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led outcome reassessment, persistent symptom documentation, safety discussion, questions, and arranged follow-up without generating treatment or a manoeuvre.
